analysis.reasoning: Core message: Lavrov jokes that the US cannot have a color revolution because it lacks an American embassy, implying the US engineers such revolutions abroad; he adds a seemingly conciliatory note about not wanting the US to fall into crisis. Evaluation by narrative: RU1 (West immoral/hostile/decadent): Yes. The joke insinuates US interference in other countries' internal affairs via color revolutions, portraying the US as a hostile, manipulative actor; this fits depicting the West (US) as immoral/hostile. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system): Not present; no promotion of multipolarity or alternative institutions. RU3 (Russia benevolent partner): Partially hinted by the line about not wanting the US to fall into crisis, but this is too weak to constitute a clear benevolent-partner framing toward others; primary thrust is accusatory toward the US, so not counted. RU4 (Russian history admirable): Not present. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront): Not present. RU6 (Russia interesting culturally/nature): Not present. Conclusion: The tweet promotes RU1 only.
